items.title: Newcastle v Aston Villa, Crystal Palace v Chelsea and more: football clockwatch – live
items.summary: ⚽ Updates from Sunday’s 2pm Premier League matches ⚽ Jonathan Wilson: is Carrick more than the new Solskjær? ⚽ Scores | Tables | Mail Daniel with any thoughts Oh, but here’s Rosenior, explaining that Cole Palmer isn’t fit to play today, but has a chance of making Wednesday’s trip to Naples. He notes that the league is close and physical, but he has good players and is excited to see where the project goes – the club “demands winning in this moment”. There aren’t many days on the training pitch, but he hopes the work they’re doing with the team and with individuals are working. Continue reading...

items.title: Sources: Fulham bid $38m for USMNT's Pepi
items.summary: Fulham have made a bid of €32 million ($38m) for the services of PSV forward Ricardo Pepi, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Van Dijk: No problem with Liverpool 'togetherness'
items.summary: Virgil van Dijk believes Liverpool lack consistency but not togetherness after the club's hopes of Champions League qualification were dealt a blow.

items.title: Rodman marks 'fresh start' in scoring U.S. return
items.summary: USWNT coach Emma Hayes heralded a "fresh start" for Trinity Rodman after the forward scored in just her second match since the 2024 Olympics in a 6-0 rout of Paraguay.

items.title: 'Best in the world' Mbappé and Vini put Madrid top
items.summary: Coach Alvaro Arbeloa praised Kylian Mbappé and Vinícius Júnior as "the best players in the world" after the pair inspired Real Madrid to a 2-0 win at Villarreal.

items.title: Messi, Miami open preseason with loss in Lima
items.summary: Lionel Messi played 63 minutes as Inter Miami CF opened its preseason with a heavy 3-0 loss to Alianza Lima in the Peruvian capital on Saturday.

items.title: VI Live: Groningen doet zichzelf tekort tegen Fortuna
items.summary: Zondag wordt er weer volop gevoetbald in binnen- en buitenland. AZ gaat om 12.15 uur op bezoek bij Telstar, FC Groningen speelt tegen Fortuna Sittard, FC Utrecht ontvangt Sparta Rotterdam en ook in het buitenland staat ons veel moois te wachten. Volg het in de VI Live.

items.title: Debuterende Dzeko scoort en helpt Schalke op weg naar cruciaal punt
items.summary: Schalke 04 blijft op koers voor een terugkeer in de Bundesliga. De ploeg uit Gelsenkirchen speelde tegen het eveneens hooggeklasseerde Kaiserslautern, en kwam in eigen huis met 0-2 achter tegen de kampioen van 1998. Schalke rechtte de rug en pakte heel laat nog een punt, mede dankzij de Anschlusstor van invaller en debutant Edin Dzeko.

items.title: VI Live: Heracles zonder Ahlstrand op jacht naar stunt in De Kuip
items.summary: Feyenoord en Heracles Almelo staan zondagmiddag om 16.45 uur tegenover elkaar in de Eredivisie. De Rotterdammers verspeelden vier competitieduels op rij punten en azen dus op eerherstel. Dat kan tegen de hekkensluiter van Nederland. Volg het duel in de VI Live.

items.title: Nani (39) plukt voetbalschoenen van de wilgen voor ijskoud Kazachs avontuur
items.summary: Nani heeft de voetbalschoenen uit het vet gehaald. De 39-jarige Portugese aanvaller speelde in november 2024 zijn laatste wedstrijd op profniveau. Nu heeft het Kazachse Aktobe hem verleid om terug te keren van zijn voetbalpensioen.

items.title: Opstelling Feyenoord: Deijl maakt meteen debuut in De Kuip
items.summary: Mats Deijl maakt zondagmiddag zijn debuut voor Feyenoord. Trainer Robin van Persie laat hem tegen Heracles Almelo direct starten.

items.title: ‘A special strike!’ | Buendia STUNS St. James’ Park with sensational long-range effort
items.summary: Emi Buendia STUNS St. James’ Park with a sensational long-range strike for Aston Villa against Newcastle in the Premier League.

items.title: Jesus starts up front for Arsenal vs unchanged Man Utd LIVE!
Manchester City go nine points clear in WSL after Shaw’s late strike at London City
items.summary: Manchester City are beginning to look like champions elect after going nine points clear at the top of the Women’s Super League with nine games to play, striking late to claim a precious victory at London City Lionesses. Khadija Shaw was the calmest person in the ground as she slotted in a low finish in the 86th minute after a goalmouth scramble, before leaping in delight in front of the travelling supporters as they celebrated her winner and avoided what had looked set to be a rare slip-up in Bromley. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Astonishing save!' | Martinez denies 'wonder' Tonali effort after just 35-seconds!
items.summary: Emi Martinez denies 'wonder' Sandro Tonali effort after just 35-seconds for Aston Villa against Newcastle in the Premier League.

items.title: Alpine confirm Horner consortium in talks to buy stake in team
items.summary: Christian Horner is in talks about an F1 comeback, with the former Red Bull team principal's consortium interested in buying a minority stake in Alpine.

items.title: Leicester City on brink of sacking Martí Cifuentes after poor run goes on
items.summary: Manager is under fierce pressure after defeat by Oxford Leicester hoped to be promotion candidates but are 14th Leicester City are on the brink of sacking Martí Cifuentes after dropping to 14th in the Championship. Leicester are to hold crunch boardroom talks on Sunday to determine the manager’s future. Leicester slumped to a humiliating home defeat against struggling Oxford United on Saturday and have won one of their past five matches. Continue reading...

items.title: Van Dijk wants 'very important' Robertson to stay
items.summary: Liverpool captain Virgil van Dijk says he wants Andy Robertson, who is a target for Tottenham, to stay at the club as he remains a "very important" member of the squad.

items.title: Why Carrick's Man Utd coaches are more than just 'a bunch of his mates'
items.summary: In his BBC Sport column, ex-Premier League boss Tony Pulis explains the thinking behind Michael Carrick's backroom staff appointments at Manchester United, and what a manager wants from those roles.

items.title: Arne Slot says Liverpool ‘ran out of energy’ in defeat at Bournemouth
items.summary: Head coach highlights away game in Champions League Slot questions fixture list after difficult away trip Arne Slot conceded his side ran out of steam in defeat at Bournemouth, after Amine Adli’s 95th-minute winner condemned Liverpool to a first loss since November. Liverpool pulled level from 2-0 down late on courtesy of Dominik Szoboszlai’s sensational free-kick, but Bournemouth responded impressively and Adli struck a winner from a long throw with almost the last kick. The Liverpool head coach felt the referee, Michael Salisbury, should have played more second-half stoppage time taking in substitutions and video assistant referee checks but admitted he feared a Bournemouth winner. “I think it is safe to say they could have scored 3-2 a little bit earlier,” Slot said, alluding to chances for the Bournemouth pair Evanilson and Ryan Christie. “A few of our players ran out of energy and I cannot even criticise them for that because two days ago [three] we had to play an away game. We’re the only team that played in the Champions League that has two games in between. Continue reading...
